title: Deutsche Telekom and AIT to Spearhead EU Quantum Communication Infrastructure via PETRUS2 and HarmoniQCI - Quantum Computing Report
gist: The European Commission awarded Deutsche Telekom and AIT Austrian Institute of Technology two Coordination and Support Actions to lead EuroQCI's next phase: Deutsche Telekom coordinates PETRUS2 (with Thales, AIT, Airbus, SES) on pan-European QKD network scaling under CEF and IRIS² funding, while AIT leads HarmoniQCI on cross-border standardization and interoperability.

insights.claim: The European Commission awarded Deutsche Telekom and AIT Austrian Institute of Technology two separate Coordination and Support Actions (CSAs) — PETRUS2 (led by Deutsche Telekom) and HarmoniQCI (led by AIT) — to transition EuroQCI from localized testbeds into a resilient, cross-border European framework.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Deutsche Telekom's continued central coordination role (following the original PETRUS program) cements it as the primary telecom operator driving EuroQCI's commercial rollout, positioning it as a key beneficiary of future EU quantum communication infrastructure spending.
insights.quote: Deutsche Telekom and the AIT Austrian Institute of Technology have been awarded two new Coordination and Support Actions (CSAs) by the European Commission to lead the development, standardization, and long-term scaling of the European Quantum Communication Infrastructure (EuroQCI).

insights.claim: PETRUS2 includes Thales, AIT, Airbus, and SES alongside Deutsche Telekom, and will coordinate cross-border Quantum Key Distribution networks funded under the EU's Connecting Europe Facility (CEF) and satellite-based ecosystem projects under the IRIS² Programme.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Names the specific consortium (Thales, AIT, Airbus, SES) positioned to capture EU funding tied to two concrete programs (CEF cross-border QKD, IRIS² satellite constellation) — a mapping of likely EuroQCI contract beneficiaries, and connects to the separately reported EAGLE-1/Airbus/SES ground-station work.
insights.quote: These include cross-border Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) networks funded under the Connecting Europe Facility (CEF) and satellite-based ecosystem projects under the IRIS² Programme.

insights.claim: HarmoniQCI, led by AIT, is specifically tasked with standardization and interoperability of quantum communication infrastructure across Europe, aiming to build a solid domestic industrial base and cohesive technical frameworks for network components and security certifications.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Explicit EU focus on standardization signals regulatory/interoperability requirements that vendors selling QKD hardware into Europe will need to comply with — a gating factor for market entry, similar to the ISO standardization dynamic seen with Post-Quantum's Classic McEliece.
insights.quote: Establishing common technical standards is crucial to building a solid domestic industrial base and preparing cohesive technical frameworks for future network components and security certifications.
